name: Medieval Speech Patterns
en:
Hark ye good fellows and attend t' me words. Pray tell, what business brings thee t' our humble kingdom? 
        By me troth, I have not seen such fine armor in many a fortnight. 
        Verily, th' king shall be most pleased with thy offerings.
        Alas, th' dreaded plague has taken many good souls from our village.
        Forsooth, 'tis a wondrous sight t' behold th' castle at dawn.
        I beseech thee, brave knight, aid us in our time o' need.
        Me liege, th' scouts have returned with dire news from th' borderlands.
        What say ye t' a flagon o' our finest mead? 'Tis brewed with honey from th' royal gardens.
        Th' lady o' th' manor requests thine presence at th' feast this eventide.
        Methinks th' storm approaches. We must seek shelter ere th' heavens open.
        Pray pardon th' intrusion, but urgent matters require thy swift attention.
        Art thou acquainted with th' legend o' th' kraken who guards th' mountain pass?
        Would that I could join thy quest, but me duties bind me t' this place.
        Perchance we shall meet again when fortune smiles upon us both.
        Th' blacksmith works yonder, should thy blade require mending.
        Hearken t' th' wisdom o' th' elders, for they have weathered many winters.
        Mayhap th' old prophecy speaks o' such troubled times as these.
        I shall return anon with provisions for thy journey.
        Fie upon these ruffians who disturb th' king's peace!
        God's wounds! What manner o' creature lurks in th' shadows?
